title: melica minor
intro: Melica minor, also known as wood melick, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Melica minor include treating conditions such as arthritis, gout, and urinary tract infections, as well as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
